name: merkle audit log: each checkpoint signature is verifiable against the corresponding public key (operator can prove integrity to auditor)
command: mkdir -p .claude-tdd-pro/keys compliance/audit-checkpoints && echo "key" > .claude-tdd-pro/keys/audit.key && bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/compliance/audit-log.sh" --append --event x --actor t --data {} --signing-key "$PWD/.claude-tdd-pro/keys/audit.key" && bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/compliance/audit-log.sh" --emit-checkpoint-now --signing-key "$PWD/.claude-tdd-pro/keys/audit.key" && bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/compliance/audit-log.sh" --verify-checkpoint compliance/audit-checkpoints/0001.json --pubkey "$PWD/.claude-tdd-pro/keys/audit.key" && echo ok 1>&2
